summary: Matched several small Sheik down-special functions in ftSk_SpecialLw, with the PR body stating the goal as matching all functions under 100 bytes. The change replaced nonmatching placeholders for animation, physics, and collision callbacks with concise C implementations, added a needed ft_0892 include and a helper declaration, and cleaned an obsolete explanatory comment in ftSeak/types.h.
what_changed: Implemented ftSk_SpecialLw_Anim: gets the Fighter from the gobj and, once ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining returns 0, assigns fp->accessory4_cb to fn_8011412C.
Implemented ftSk_SpecialLw2_Anim: when animation frames are exhausted, calls ft_8008A2BC on the Fighter_GObj.

Implemented ftSk_SpecialAirLw2_Phys: reads ftSeakAttributes from fp->dat_attrs, includes a local u8 padding/scratch array, then calls ftCommon_8007D494 with attributes->x68 and attributes->x6C followed by ftCommon_8007CEF4.
Implemented ftSk_SpecialLw2_Coll: checks ft_80082708 for GA_Ground and calls ftSk_SpecialLw_80114680 when grounded.
Added #include "ft/ft_0892.h" to ftSk_SpecialLw.c, likely to provide declarations used by the newly matched code.
Added a declaration for ftSk_SpecialLw_80114680 in ftSk_SpecialLw.h.

Removed an old comment in ftSeak/types.h about adding fields and shrinking data_filler_1 based on a decomp.me scratch for ftSk_SpecialAirHiStart_1_Coll; the actual struct fields remained in place in the excerpt.
smart_moves: Targeted very small functions under 100 bytes, which is an efficient way to reduce nonmatching placeholders with low-risk, localized callback logic.

Used existing engine helper calls rather than open-coding behavior: ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining for animation completion, ft_8008A2BC for transition/ending behavior, ftCommon_8007D494 and ftCommon_8007CEF4 for aerial physics handling, and ft_80082708 for ground-state collision checks.
Preserved callback semantics by assigning accessory4_cb only when the transform-start ground animation has no remaining frames.
Kept type casts explicit between HSD_GObj, Fighter_GObj, and Fighter where required by existing helper signatures.
Included the seemingly unused local u8 _[4] in ftSk_SpecialAirLw2_Phys, suggesting a stack-layout or register-allocation tactic needed for matching.
decomp_lessons: For fighter animation callbacks, a common matching pattern is to gate follow-up behavior on ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining((Fighter_GObj*) gobj) == 0.
For simple collision callbacks, matching code may reduce to checking ft_80082708((Fighter_GObj*) gobj) against GA_Ground and dispatching to a ground-transition helper.
When matching small physics functions, access character-specific attributes through fp->dat_attrs and use existing ftCommon physics helpers with attribute fields rather than duplicating calculations.
Do not remove odd-looking local dummy arrays too quickly; the u8 _[4] in ftSk_SpecialAirLw2_Phys is likely present for stack/register matching despite having no semantic use in the excerpt.
Once provisional struct documentation becomes stale or no longer useful, cleanup in types.h can make the header less misleading while keeping the actual recovered fields.
naming_conventions: Sheik-specific fighter files use the ftSk_ prefix for functions and ftSeak for the character directory/types naming.
Action-state callback suffixes follow the standard fighter convention: _Anim, _Phys, _Coll, and _IASA.
Unidentified or address-based helper functions retain numeric suffix names such as ftSk_SpecialLw_80114680 and ftSk_SpecialLw_801146EC.
Recovered attribute fields in ftSeakAttributes remain offset-style names such as x68 and x6C when their semantic names are not yet known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched nonmatching placeholders by implementing minimal control-flow bodies corresponding to animation-end, physics, and collision callback idioms.
Used explicit casts to Fighter_GObj* for helpers expecting fighter object wrappers and GET_FIGHTER for direct Fighter* access.
Introduced a local u8 _[4] scratch/padding variable in ftSk_SpecialAirLw2_Phys, likely to influence stack layout for an exact match.
Added missing declarations/includes alongside implementation so newly referenced functions compile cleanly without implicit declarations.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human body text states: "Matching all functions under 100 bytes."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftSeakAttributes fields x68 and x6C to semantic names.
Search for other fighter special callbacks using u8 _[4] or dummy stack padding to match physics functions.
Compare ftSk_SpecialLw2_Coll with Zelda or other transform move collision callbacks for shared transition patterns.
Look for follow-up PRs that match the remaining ftSk_SpecialLw placeholders such as ftSk_SpecialAirLw_Enter or ftSk_SpecialLw_801146EC.
